title: Green Plains Achieves Carbon Capture Milestone; Targets $188M in 45Z Credits for 2026
content: Green Plains Inc. has successfully operationalized carbon capture facilities at its Central City, Wood River, and York, Nebraska plants, significantly lowering the carbon intensity of its ethanol production. This milestone allowed the company to realize $23.4 million in value from the 45Z Clean Fuel Production Tax Credit (net of discounts) within its fourth-quarter Adjusted EBITDA. For the full year 2025, the company recorded a $54.2 million income tax benefit related to these credits. Looking ahead, Green Plains forecasts generating at least $188 million in 45Z-related Adjusted EBITDA in 2026, driven by continued low-carbon production. The company is actively marketing these 2026 credits and has already executed monetization agreements for its "Advantage Nebraska" sites. This shift to lower-carbon intensity ethanol positions Green Plains to supply emerging markets, including feedstock for Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF).
author: Analysts Account
